a_image.alt: Library Bookshelf UI style mood board — Skeuomorphic · Literary

sections.a_description: Dark mahogany shelves, leather-bound spines, and the warm glow of a desk lamp on cream parchment — Library Bookshelf UI recreates the reverent atmosphere of a private study or rare book room. The rich wood tones, elegant serif typography, and parchment-colored surfaces evoke the sensory experience of pulling a cherished volume from the shelf and settling into a leather armchair to read.

This aesthetic appears on digital libraries, book club platforms, and literary journal websites. You'll find it on antiquarian bookshop sites, reading app interfaces, university archive portals, and any project that wants to surround its content with the warmth and intellectual gravitas of a well-curated personal library.

sections.a_description: A warm, scholarly library atmosphere — dark mahogany wood, leather-bound volumes, warm lamplight, and the reverent quiet of a reading room. Background: rich dark wood (#2c1810) with a subtle vertical wood-grain texture via repeating-linear-gradient. The .inner panel simulates an open book page: cream/parchment (#faf5eb) background with a slight inner shadow on the left side (as if the page meets the book spine), and a subtle fold line. Typography is elegant and bookish. Set in Cormorant Garamond for headings — a high-contrast display Garamond with sharp serifs and elegant proportions, perfect for chapter titles. Body in Lora — a balanced contemporary serif optimized for screen reading. Heading at weight 600, 30px, line-height 1.2, letter-spacing -0.3px, color #2c1810 (matching the wood). Eyebrow at 12px Lora italic, weight 400, color #8b6f47 (warm leather brown), no uppercase. Body at 15px Lora, weight 400, line-height 1.8, color #3e3028. Buttons: primary — dark wood (#2c1810) background, cream text, 4px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, warm brown border. Alternatives: EB Garamond (classical Garamond), Crimson Text (humanist serif), Libre Baskerville (transitional).
